name: CRUD/delete
description: Instance delete (DELETE) conformance tests: a deleted resource returns 410/404 on read while its pre-delete version remains vread-able and it is excluded from search (from DeleteTests). Hard delete and $purge-history — both server extensions not mandated by base FHIR R4 — are covered separately in Microsoft/ms-hard-delete-purge-history.json.

variable.name: deleteBasicId
variable.description: server-assigned id of the basic delete-lifecycle Patient

variable.name: deleteBasicVersion
variable.description: server-assigned initial versionId of the basic delete-lifecycle Patient, expected to remain vread-able after delete

variable.name: deleteSearchId
variable.description: server-assigned id of the Patient used for the deleted-excluded-from-search test

test.name: delete removes the resource; a plain read is Gone/NotFound while the original version remains vread-able
test.description: DELETE Patient/{id} must succeed. A subsequent plain read must return 410 Gone (or 404 Not Found as a warningOnly alternative), while a vread of the version that existed before the delete must still succeed.

operation.description: Create the Patient that will be deleted

assert.description: Delete must return a 2xx success status

operation.description: Attempt to read the deleted Patient

assert.description: Preferred: 410 Gone for a deleted resource (warningOnly: FHIR also permits 404 when deleted resources are not tracked)
Alternative: 404 Not Found is accepted when the server does not track deleted resources

operation.description: vread the pre-delete version of the Patient

assert.description: vread of the pre-delete version must still return HTTP 200
Response body must be a Patient
Returned id must match the deleted Patient's id

test.name: deleted resource does not appear in subsequent searches
test.description: A Patient tagged with a unique identifier is created, confirmed searchable, deleted, and then confirmed absent from the same identifier search.

operation.description: Create the Patient that will be searched for, then deleted
Search by identifier before delete; the Patient must be present

assert.description: Must include the created Patient before delete
Delete must return a 2xx success status

operation.description: Search by the same identifier after delete; the Patient must be excluded

assert.description: Must NOT include the deleted Patient after delete

operation.description: Teardown: delete the basic delete-lifecycle Patient (already deleted by the test, best effort)
Teardown: delete the search-exclusion Patient (already deleted by the test, best effort)
